comparison_surface_note: Compares official Login clientbound custom_query framed packet id and body by splitting the generated frame and dispatching the official body bytes through Stevenarella packet_by_id.
test_topology_note: The target file and test name are this manifest's current adapter, not the universal runner topology for all oracle cases.

official_source.function_or_member: Identifier.parse(String), DiscardedQueryPayload(Identifier), ClientboundCustomQueryPacket(int, CustomQueryPayload), ClientboundCustomQueryPacket.STREAM_CODEC, FriendlyByteBuf.readVarInt/writeVarInt, FriendlyByteBuf.readIdentifier/writeIdentifier, LoginProtocols.CLIENTBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ClientboundCustomQueryPacket), LoginProtocols.CLIENTBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets(...), ClientboundCustomQueryPacket.transactionId(), ClientboundCustomQueryPacket.payload(), DiscardedQueryPayload.id(), CustomQueryPayload.write(FriendlyByteBuf), ClientLoginPacketListener.handleCustomQuery(ClientboundCustomQueryPacket)

internal_owner.symbol_or_test_surface: packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Login, Direction::Clientbound, official packet id, official body)
internal_owner.scope_note: Current implementation witness for Login clientbound custom_query framed dispatch/decode; not plugin channel handling, custom query semantics, login acknowledgement behavior, Login-to-Configuration state transition handling, Configuration entry, Play readiness, or client-load completion.

stop_boundary: This manifest proves Login clientbound custom_query packet framing and dispatch/decode for one official transactionId=0, payloadId=a:a, empty DiscardedQueryPayload fixture only; it does not prove plugin channel handling, custom query semantics, login acknowledgement behavior, Login-to-Configuration state transition handling, Configuration entry, Play readiness, or client-load completion.
